Title: Adjusting the scent ratio: using genetically modified Vitis vinifera plants to manipulate European grapevine moth behaviour

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpression can be used to increase (E)-β-caryophyllene emission and alter the (E)-β-caryophyllene/(E)-β-farnesene ratio in elite grapevine backgrounds for behavioural interference with Lobesia botrana. Best used with ratio-based engineering rather than single-compound maximization alone, and likely in IPM rather than as a stand-alone strategy. Caution: varietal background affects baseline ratios, beneficial insects may also respond, and insect adaptation/learning may reduce long-term efficacy.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes/transforms farnesyl diphosphate into (E)-β-caryophyllene as a major sesquiterpene product.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased (E)-β-caryophyllene emission in grapevine without significantly changing (E)-β-farnesene, thereby shifting the volatile ratio and reducing moth attraction when the ratio became strongly divergent from control plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic overexpression in grapevine; volatile profiling by SPME-GC-MS and CLSA-GC-MS; comparison of transcript abundance with RT-qPCR; behavioural validation in wind tunnel assays using plant extracts and whole plants.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse-acclimatized plants; VOC sampling standardized by plant age/size and time of day to minimize diel effects.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reported previously as the main source of (E)-β-caryophyllene production in green tissues of grapevine.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Responsible for most volatile production in green tissues of grapevine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RNAi silencing was attempted but no significantly silenced lines were obtained, likely due to high sequence similarity among grapevine TPS family members. Overexpression effects on metabolite output were only poorly correlated with transcript level. DMNT emission was unchanged.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth or phenotype differences were observed in transgenic lines under greenhouse conditions. Potential ecological tradeoffs discussed include effects on non-target/beneficial insects and possible pest adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; acts as a key determinant of volatile blend ratio in this system
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvGwECar2 was selected because it is the main grapevine gene responsible for (E)-β-caryophyllene production in green tissues. 2. Stable overexpression increased (E)-β-caryophyllene emission up to about 20 ng/g fresh weight in young leaves and altered whole-plant volatile ratios. 3. Strongly altered (E)-β-caryophyllene/(E)-β-farnesene ratios reduced attraction of Lobesia botrana females, supporting causal involvement of this enzyme in ratio-dependent ecological signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Young leaves and whole above-ground plant headspace
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant volatile involved in host-location cues (kairomonal blend) affecting insect behaviour; can contribute to ecological interactions and pest management strategies.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Heterologous expression provides a practical route to engineer grapevine for elevated (E)-β-farnesene emission where no native dedicated synthase is known. Useful for tuning volatile ratios in elite cultivars for pest-behaviour manipulation, preferably combined with IPM and careful ecological assessment.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive expression strongly increased (E)-β-farnesene emission, up to about 20-fold, without significantly affecting (E)-β-caryophyllene, thereby lowering the volatile ratio and reducing moth attraction when the shift was large.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning from Artemisia annua; stable Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of grapevine; RT-qPCR of transgene expression; volatile profiling by SPME-GC-MS and CLSA-GC-MS; behavioural validation in wind tunnel assays using extracts, synthetic blends, and whole plants.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse-acclimatized plants; VOC collections standardized for plant age/size and timing to limit diel variation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expression measured in young grapevine leaves; expression varied strongly among independent lines.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cloned from mature leaves of Artemisia annua.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors state no known dedicated (E)-β-farnesene synthase exists in the grapevine genome; endogenous grapevine (E)-β-farnesene is a minor by-product of other sesquiterpene synthases. Transcript level differed by ~30-fold among lines and showed a moderate positive correlation with metabolite accumulation (Pearson r = 0.65). DMNT emission remained unchanged.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth or phenotype differences were observed in transgenic plants. Potential ecological risks discussed include altered responses of beneficial insects and pest adaptation/learning.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; functions as a key determinant of volatile blend ratio in engineered grapevine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Aab-FS was introduced because grapevine lacks a known dedicated (E)-β-farnesene synthase. 2. CaMV 35S-driven expression in grapevine produced up to ~20-fold higher (E)-β-farnesene emission and shifted the (E)-β-caryophyllene/(E)-β-farnesene ratio. 3. Whole-plant extracts, synthetic blends mimicking engineered ratios, and intact plants all showed reduced Lobesia botrana attraction when the ratio was strongly altered, linking Aab-FS-mediated metabolic change to the ecological phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Young leaves and whole above-ground plant headspace
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant volatile involved in host-location cues (kairomonal blend) affecting insect behaviour; relevant for ecological interactions and pest management.
